description: This figure presents post-tensile characterization of a low-density Fe-Mn-Al-Ti-B steel containing TiB₂, combining EBSD and TEM bright-field imaging. Panels (a–a₂) display pseudo-3D EBSD micrographs: (a) an IPF map showing grain orientations relative to RD, TD, and ND; (a₁) a phase map distinguishing BCC δ-ferrite, FCC austenite, and TiB₂ constituents; and (a₂) a KAM map revealing local misorientation distributions (50 μm scale). Panels (b–b₂) show TEM images of the BCC δ-ferrite phase: (b) a grain away from TiB₂ particles (per caption) with a BCC <110> zone-axis SADP; (b₁) dense dislocation pile-ups and tangles at a δ-ferrite/TiB₂ interface; and (b₂) the corresponding SADP with g = 011. Panels (c–c₂) show the FCC austenite phase: (c) a grain away from TiB₂ (per caption) with an FCC <001> zone-axis SADP; (c₁) dislocation pile-ups with comparatively sparse tangles at an austenite/TiB₂ interface; and (c₂) the corresponding SADP with g = 111 (200 nm scale). The micrographs demonstrate significantly higher dislocation accumulation at δ-ferrite/TiB₂ interfaces than at austenite/TiB₂ interfaces, supporting a synergistic deformation mechanism where δ-ferrite provides a stable strengthening framework and austenite contributes increasing work hardening.
